summary: Backend logging persistence system successfully implemented with high code quality
strengths: Clean modular design - logging module properly separated with clear public API
Comprehensive test coverage - 4 unit tests, all passing, covering core functionality
No clippy warnings in logging.rs - code follows Rust best practices
Proper error handling with Result types throughout
Excellent Chinese comments explaining purpose and implementation
Security compliance - no sensitive data (passwords/keys) logged
Daily log rotation with 7-day retention correctly implemented
Separate error.log output using tracing layers
System info generation with proper metadata
Integration follows project architecture (uses get_data_dir() from Issue #43)

issues.description: Two unused helper functions in test module (setup_test_log_dir, cleanup_test_dir)
issues.impact: Generates dead_code warnings, but doesn't affect functionality
issues.recommendation: Remove unused test helpers or mark with #[allow(dead_code)]

issues.impact: Blocks clippy --all from passing, but not related to this PR
issues.recommendation: Should be fixed in a separate cleanup commit

compliance.architecture: ✅ Follows ADR-006 unified data directory pattern
compliance.coding_style: ✅ Chinese comments, clear naming, follows personal-style.md
compliance.security: ✅ No sensitive information logged (verified with grep)
compliance.testing: ✅ 4 tests passing, proper use of tempfile for test isolation

verification.plan_alignment: ✅ All 8 tasks completed according to issue-44-plan.md

before_merge: Remove unused test helper functions or add #[allow(dead_code)]
Consider running manual test to verify log files are created on app startup
Verify error.log only contains ERROR level messages
future_improvements: Consider adding log level configuration via IPC command (for debug mode)
Add integration test that actually runs the app and verifies log creation
Consider adding log file size monitoring (mentioned in plan risk mitigation)
